title: event — compliance evidence
description: Event is a scheduling + commercial container. Bulk of compliance responsibility: financial-record retention (entry_fee / late_fee / prize_pool changes -> 7-year audit rows), SOC 2 CC6.3 change tracking (EventCancellationBroadcast + EventRescheduled + EventVenueChanged + EventPricingChanged + EventMinorEligibilityCorrected are the highest-severity events), and child-safeguarding regulator interest on events with minor registrants.

controls.evidence: Event fields used only for scheduling + registration + reporting + entitlement enforcement. Never resold, never joined with external enrichment.
Archived Events retained 2555 days (7 years — financial retention floor). Financial audit rows outlive the source Event.

Every Event mutation is Auditable. Status transitions, cancellations, pricing changes, eligibility narrowings — every one writes to `audits` with actor + timestamp + changed_fields + request_id + before/after snapshots for financial fields.
Every write behind EventPolicy. Lifecycle transitions carry distinct permissions. Sensitive post-registration mutations (dates.shift, venue.change, pricing.change, eligibility.narrow) are owner-only.

controls.principle: authorisation of changes to system components
controls.evidence: EventCancellationBroadcast + EventRescheduled + EventVenueChanged + EventPricingChanged + EventMinorEligibilityCorrected are top audit-critical events — every fire writes to `audits` with 7-year retention + fires cannot-opt-out notifications to affected parties.
Health probes cover FK integrity, phase-roll heartbeat, reconciliation-last-run marker, event_facilities ↔ resource_bookings drift, and per-hour phase-transition counts.

Event.entry_fee_cents + late_registration_fee_cents + prize_pool_cents changes generate financial-record classified audit rows retained 7 years. Post-registration_open changes are refused without the events.pricing.change permission (owner-only) + fire EventPricingChanged with audit_severity='critical' + cannot-opt-out notification to registered participants disclosing the change + refund posture.

EventCancelled + EventCancellationBroadcast retain 7-year audit records including cancellation_reason + registered_count_at_cancel. The refund liability trail is preserved regardless of the event's row deletion (audit outlives the row).

controls.provision: advance-notice for schedule changes on events with minors
controls.evidence: For any Event where the registrant pool includes minors (Wave 3+ enrollment carries the age band), EventRescheduled + EventVenueChanged + EventCancellationBroadcast + EventPricingChanged CC guardians via a mandatory cannot-opt-out channel. Regulators (US state childcare regs, UK Ofsted, EU child-protection directives) treat surprise schedule/venue/cancellation on minors' events as an audit event.

EventMinorEligibilityCorrected fires with critical severity when narrowing min_age/max_age/age_group_ids excludes existing registrants. Excluded minor registrants + their guardians receive a cannot-opt-out notification + refund posture. The excluded_minor_count field on the audit row is regulator-facing.

controls.provision: livestream password confidentiality on events with minors
controls.evidence: livestream_password is classified confidential (data-classes.json). Never rendered in any in-app UI or public wire. Delivered exclusively via secure email + push to registered participants + guardians. Prevents scraping of live footage of minors.

Aliased to GDPR Art. 17. Event rows not directly PII; tenant erasure cascades. Individual participant deletions handled by Wave 3 athlete-enrollment — those deletions leave Event intact but remove the registration.

pci_dss.notes: Event does not store payment card data. Wave 4 finance module owns invoicing per registration + integrates with the payment gateway; Event only holds fee amount + currency.
